rationale: Empirically verified in the bead worktree. `go build ./...` and `go vet ./...` both exit 0; all five target suites PASS (`internal/layout` 17 tests, plus gitutil/executor/doctor/cmd/mindspec) under -count=1; gofmt -l on the 16 changed .go files is empty. Harness suite correctly not run. Inspected the mover tests for GENUINE coverage — none are asserted-but-untested: TwoCommitPerMove proves a pure R100 rename commit FIRST then a separate content-only rewrite commit with `git log --follow` surviving (Deviation C honored); GoldenFlatten asserts R100 on the specs move + follow-survival + idempotent re-run (HEAD unchanged); RollbackOnFailure injects failure after the group-1 move-commit and asserts HEAD==preRun with the canonical tree intact; CrashResumeEveryBoundary crashes+resumes at all six run-state boundaries; both link-check tests assert *LinkCheckError (dangling-rewritten also asserts rollback; un-rewritten bare `docs/` form proves every-link scanning). LineageManifest parses the ACTUAL written manifest, and the doctor-side TestLineageManifestParsesUnderSchema drives the pre-existing checkMigrationMetadata against json tags (source/canonical/archive, run_id/stage) verified to match. Mover only operates on its `root` param (every test uses t.TempDir()); the live .mindspec/docs is never touched — the `.mindspec/docs` literals are move-plan constants/help text only.

findings.issue: go build, go vet exit 0; gofmt -l empty; all 5 target suites PASS (-count=1). internal/harness intentionally not run.

TestMover_TwoCommitPerMove asserts a pure 100%-rename commit (R100 name-status) then a distinct link-rewrite commit (content change to moved/a.md, NOT R100) and git log --follow survival — Deviation C's per-move protocol is genuinely proven, not just stated.

Idempotent re-run, hard-reset rollback (fail after a landed move commit), crash-resume at all six boundaries, both link-check FAIL paths (dangling-rewritten + un-rewritten breaking), and lineage-manifest-under-doctor-schema are each backed by real assertions; no unbacked correctness claim found.

Mover acts only on its root param; all golden/crash/rollback tests use t.TempDir(). The live .mindspec/docs tree is never mutated by this bead — confirmed (the .mindspec/docs references are DefaultFlattenPlan/RewriteRule constants and CLI help text).

doctor TestLineageManifestParsesUnderSchema uses a hand-written manifest fixture rather than invoking the mover, so it is a contract test; coupling is mitigated because layout TestMover_LineageManifest parses the actually-written manifest and the json struct tags are verified identical to the doctor schema. Acceptable; non-blocking.

Crash-resume covers stageFinalize at group -1 but not the stageRootRewrite (-1) boundary explicitly; root-doc rewrite is idempotent and the finalize-resume path exercises the post-root-rewrite resume, so the gap is immaterial. Non-blocking.
